stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Eight species of pangolin exist today.
  • Four pangolin species live in sub-Saharan Africa.
  • One of those species is the giant pangolin.
  • Its scientific name is Smutsia gigantea.
  • Another of those species is the white-bellied pangolin.
  • Its scientific name is Phataginus tricuspis.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two pangolin species. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Both the giant pangolin and the white-bellied pangolin live in sub-Saharan Africa.
The giant pangolin (Smutsia gigantea) and the white-bellied pangolin (Phataginus tricuspis) are different species.
The giant pangolin is referred to as Smutsia gigantea, while the white-bellied pangolin is referred to as Phataginus tricuspis.
Among the eight species of pangolin is the white-bellied pangolin, which lives in sub-Saharan Africa.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between the two pangolin species: both live in sub-Saharan Africa.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference between the two species rather than a similarity. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference between the two species rather than a similarity. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes only the white-bellied pangolin; it fails to mention the giant pangolin.
